data.title: Haig Partners Serves as Exclusive Advisor on the Divestiture of Hello Auto Group’s Valencia Platform
data.description:
Haig Partners Serves as Exclusive Advisor on the Divestiture of Hello Auto Group’s Valencia Platform

Transactions Include the Sale of Hello Subaru of Valencia, Hello Kia of Valencia, and Hello Mazda of Valencia in the Valencia Auto Mall

Haig Partners LLC, a leading buy-sell advisory firm to auto retailers in the United States, served as the exclusive sell-side advisor to Hello Auto Group on the sale of its Santa Clarita platform located in the Valencia Auto Mall, consisting of Hello Subaru of Valencia, Hello Kia of Valencia, and Hello Mazda of Valencia.

The transactions represent a strategic repositioning by Hello Auto Group as it continues to optimize its portfolio and focus on long-term growth initiatives. Haig Partners advised the company and its Board of Directors throughout a coordinated, multi-transaction process.

Hello Subaru of Valencia was acquired by Jerry Seiner Dealerships, an established and expanding dealership group with operations across the Western United States. Hello Mazda of Valencia and Hello Kia of Valencia were acquired by Ghreiwati Auto Group, a diversified operator with an established presence in Southern California.

California continues to attract significant interest from sophisticated buyers, both domestic and international. Demand has been supported by improving regulatory clarity, including developments related to CARB, as well as the state’s position as the largest automotive market in the United States and one of the largest economies globally. Continued economic strength, supported in part by growth in AI and technology, has reinforced buyer confidence and transaction activity. Buyers who have invested in California in prior cycles are benefiting today, and sellers entering the market are well positioned to achieve strong outcomes.

“It was a privilege to represent Hello Auto Group and its Board in this strategic divestiture,” said Jayson Crouch, Managing Director at Haig Partners. “We brought the assets to market during a dynamic period for buy-sell activity in California, and as clarity improved around CARB and broader industry factors, including tariffs, buyer demand and valuations strengthened meaningfully.

We ran a disciplined, competitive process with a broad group of sophisticated buyers across the country. By maintaining control of the process and aligning timing with market conditions, we maximized competitive tension and delivered an outcome that reflected the full value of the assets and exceeded our client’s expectations.”

“Alan Haig, Jayson Crouch and the Haig Partners team did a fantastic job representing the interests of all board members and securing an excellent outcome,” said Paul Walser, Partner and Board Member of Hello Auto Group and Partner of Walser Automotive Group. “They ran a confidential and disciplined process, executed every step with professionalism and maintained alignment across a complex group of stakeholders. They never overpromised but delivered a result that reflected the strength of the business while maintaining transparency and integrity throughout.”

“Alan Haig and Jayson Crouch ran a complete process for us as we divested the Santa Clarita platform and repositioned the company for the future,” said Pat Denzer, Partner and Board Member of Hello Auto Group. “They were trusted advisors who kept us informed throughout the process and ultimately delivered an outcome that exceeded shareholder expectations.”

“I would like to thank Jayson Crouch, Alan Haig, and the entire team at Haig Partners for their hard work and professionalism in completing the transactions involving our stores in Valencia, California,” said Karl Schmidt, President of Hello Auto Group. “The process was completed in three segments with two buyers as part of the divestiture of our Valencia platform. The team ran a thorough and disciplined process that generated strong interest and delivered an outcome that exceeded our expectations. They were professional, detailed, responsive, and respectful of all parties’ interests throughout.”

Dealers interested in learning more about buying or selling a dealership in California should contact Jayson Crouch, Managing Director at Haig Partners, at Jayson@HaigPartners.com.

About Haig Partners

Haig Partners is a leading buy-sell advisory firm that helps owners of higher-value dealerships maximize the value of their businesses when they are ready to sell. The team at Haig Partners has advised on the purchase or sale of more than 580 dealerships and has represented 32 dealership groups that qualify for the Top 150 Dealership Groups list published by Automotive News, more than any other firm.

Clients of Haig Partners benefit from the group’s collective experience as former executives with leading companies such as AutoNation, Bank of America, Credit Suisse, Deloitte, FORVIS, J.P. Morgan, Lexus, Porsche, and Toyota Financial Services. Leveraging its unmatched expertise and extensive relationships, Haig Partners guides clients to successful outcomes through a confidential and customized sales process.

The firm authors The Haig Report®, the leading quarterly report tracking trends in auto retail and their impact on dealership values, and co-authors NADA’s Guide, Buying and Selling a Dealership. Haig Partners team members are frequent speakers at industry conferences and are regularly quoted in Reuters, Forbes, The Wall Street Journal, The New York Times, CNBC, BBC, Automotive News, Wards, and CarDealershipGuy. For more information, https://www.haigpartners.com.

data.title: Mortgage rates rise for the fifth week in a row. Here's how much more homebuyers are paying since the Iran war's start.
data.description:
Andrew Keshner

One year ago, the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage averaged 6.64%

Home buyers have encountered higher borrowing costs in recent weeks.

Mortgage rates have continued to climb as the war in Iran drags on, increasing for the fifth consecutive week and reaching their highest point since early September.

The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage reached 6.46%, up from 6.38% a week ago, Freddie Mac (FMCC) said Thursday. However, that's still below the 6.64% level where it was at this time last year.

Mortgage rates tend to follow the yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y, which was lower on Thursday, at 4.304%, at last check.

The new mortgage-rate data come on the heels of President Donald Trump's national address Wednesday night, in which he emphasized the U.S. military's accomplishments against the Iranian regime. Operation Epic Fury's "core strategic objectives are nearing completion," Trump said. Stock markets fell in response, after climbing in recent days on hopes for an end in the fighting.

Gas prices will fall quickly after the war ends, Trump said. Since the conflict was launched by the U.S. and Israel on Feb. 28, prices at the pump have jumped by $1 a gallon, mirroring the swift rise in mortgage costs potential home buyers are seeing. Some simple math shows the price of the recent rate run-up.

Just before the start of fighting in the Middle East, mortgage rates had dropped to their lowest level since 2022, with the 30-year rate at 5.98% on Feb. 26, Freddie Mac data show.

Someone buying a home this week for $398,000 - the median sales price of an existing home in February, according to the National Association of Realtors - with a 20% down payment and a 6.46% mortgage rate would pay an estimated $2,383 per month, according to a Zillow (Z) mortgage calculator. (The calculation includes an estimate of monthly expenses for taxes and insurance.)

That's essentially $100 more per month than they would have paid if the same sale had occurred in late February at a 5.98% mortgage rate, the Zillow calculator shows.

Over the life of the loan, that's approximately $36,000 more paid toward interest, the calculator shows.

Last month, the median monthly mortgage payment was $2,742, according to Redfin (RKT). It was a small year-over-year increase of 0.3%, but it was the first increase since October, the analysis showed.

There's been a large run-up in rates in a short period, but that's not historically unprecedented, according to Jeff DerGurahian, chief investment officer and head economist at loanDepot (LDI).

Borrowing costs are a major factor in a home buyer's decision, but they are not the only factor. It's too soon to say how the mortgage-rate increases will affect the spring home-buying season, he noted.

The March jobs report, due out Friday, "has the potential to matter more than most of the data releases we've seen since this conflict began," he said.

Also read: The U.S. isn't creating many jobs anymore. The March jobs report won't buck the trend.

A below-forecast jobs number would put more focus on the economy's "underlying softness" and "help improve the outlook for rates, although mortgage rates may not improve as quickly as Treasury yields," DerGurahian said. But an above-forecast number "could reinforce the idea that borrowing costs may stay elevated for longer."

Either way, DerGurahian said, "bringing mortgage rates meaningfully back toward 6% is unlikely to happen overnight. It will likely take more than one report, along with some easing in the Middle East, before we see a more durable move lower."

-Andrew Keshner

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  04-02-26 1209ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Space 11 Completes Global Space Advisory Board
data.description:
The completion of the Global Space Advisory Board was announced by Bert Ulrich, Executive Vice President of Production, from Florida's space coast, highlighting Space 11's proximity to active spaceflight operations and the broader commercial space ecosystem.

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 2, 2026 / Space 11, a space economy, infrastructure and technology company, today announced the completion of its Global Space Advisory Board, formalizing a senior advisory structure composed of former NASA leaders and industry specialists to support its expansion across space-based media, infrastructure and emerging commercial space initiatives.

The announcement was made by Bert Ulrich, Executive Vice President of Production, from Florida's space coast, where he attended the Artemis II launch, reflecting Space 11's positioning in close proximity to active spaceflight operations and the broader entertainment and commercial space ecosystem.

The advisory board includes former NASA astronaut Scott Kelly, one of the most experienced spaceflight veterans of his generation and the first American astronaut to spend nearly a year aboard the International Space Station; former NASA Chief Scientist Dr. James L. Green, who helped define the agency's long-term scientific strategy and oversaw planetary exploration programs including missions to Mars and deep space; former senior NASA executive Dr. Camille Wardrop Alleyne, who held leadership roles across human spaceflight, science missions and the development of the commercial low-Earth orbit economy; and Bert Ulrich, former NASA Film and Television Liaison, who spent more than two decades bridging the agency with global media productions and now serves also as Executive Vice President of Production at Space 11.

The completion of the board represents a structural step in the company's evolution, aligning its development strategy with current aerospace capabilities, scientific standards and the accelerating commercialization of space.

Space 11 was founded in 2021 by Italian producer and entrepreneur Andrea Iervolino, whose international film productions have involved Academy Award-winning filmmakers and globally recognized talent. His work has focused on structuring large-scale, cross-border production platforms, a model now extended into Space 11's activities across space economy infrastructure, technology and media.

The company is currently developing "I See You", a feature film project designed to incorporate scenes captured in real microgravity conditions. The film is written by Andrew Schneider and Diane Frolov and is structured to combine traditional production with footage obtained during human spaceflight, with the objective of achieving a higher level of realism in the depiction of life in orbit.

The company's broader strategy includes the development of media formats, sports competitions and content designed for execution in space environments, supported by collaborations with aerospace engineers, astronauts and launch providers.

Andrea Iervolino, Founder, stated:

"The completion of our Global Space Advisory Board marks an important step in the development of Space 11 as a platform operating across space economy infrastructure, technology and media. We have brought together individuals who have directly contributed to shaping modern space exploration, and their expertise allows us to structure projects that are both visionary and grounded in real operational capability."

Press Office:
Ludovica Contu
+447748408619
ludovica.contu@hotmail.it

SOURCE: Space 11 Corp.



View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Excella’s Fraud AI Solution Deemed “Awardable” in the CDAO’s Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace
data.description:
Excella’s Fraud AI Solution Deemed “Awardable” in the CDAO’s Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace

Excella to offer AI-powered fraud detection and prevention solution to federal agencies

Excella, a transformative technology firm that helps organizations create high-performing technology, today announced that its solution, Excella Fraud AnalytiX, has achieved “Awardable” status through the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office’s (CDAO) Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace.

The Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace is the premier offering of Tradewinds, the Department of Defense’s (DoD’s) suite of tools and services designed to accelerate the procurement and adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI)/Machine Learning (ML), data, and analytics capabilities.

“The Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace is exactly the kind of vehicle that connects proven solutions to mission-ready buyers,” said Tammy McChain, Vice President of Strategic Solutions and Growth at Excella. “An awardable listing gives us a direct, streamlined path to deliver Excella's fraud detection capability to federal customers who need it.”

Excella's solutions are designed to challenge what's expected when it comes to delivery in AI & Analytics, Modern Software Delivery, and Organizational Transformation. They are used by a wide range of businesses, including Fortune 500 companies, national nonprofits, small businesses, and government agencies. Excella’s solution, Excella Fraud AnalytiX, detects fraud in the seams between systems and empowers investigators to prevent it.

“For decades, Excella has helped government customers detect and prevent fraud in important systems,” said Burton White, Co-Founder and CEO of Excella. “We’re excited to offer this proven solution to more government customers through the Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace. I am excited to see the impact Excella Fraud AnalytiX will have on more government missions.”

Excella was recognized among a competitive field of applicants to the Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace whose solutions demonstrated innovation, scalability, and potential impact. Government customers interested in viewing the video solution can create a Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace account at tradewindAI.com.

About Excella

Excella is a transformative technology firm that helps organizations unlock new possibilities. Driven by our clients’ missions, we deliver high-performing technology solutions through Modern Software Delivery, Artificial Intelligence and Analytics, and Organizational Transformation. Our exceptional teams partner with organizations to tackle their most critical challenges, creating solutions that power mission efficiency today and into tomorrow. Founded in 2002, Excella continues to help clients build robust technology foundations while strengthening their ability to meet tomorrow’s challenges. A recognized industry leader, Excella has been named among Forbes’ America’s Top Management Consulting Firms and Washington Post’s Top Places to Work. Learn more at www.excella.com.

About the Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace

The Tradewinds Solutions Marketplace is a digital repository of post-competition, readily awardable pitch videos that address the Department of Defense’s (DoD) most significant challenges in the Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML), data, and analytics space. All awardable solutions have been assessed through complex scoring rubrics and competitive procedures and are available to Government customers with a Marketplace account. Government customers can create an account at www.tradewindai.com. Tradewinds is housed in the DoD’s Chief Digital Artificial Intelligence Office.

data.title: Trump Administration to Appeal Injuction in Anthropic Court Battle
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall 




  The Trump administration will appeal a U.S. federal judge's decision to halt the designation of Anthropic as a supply-chain risk, according to a Thursday court filing. 



  The filing indicates that the administration is ready to continue a court battle with the artificial-intelligence company, which filed a lawsuit against the Department of Defense early last month over the designation. 



  Judge Rita F. Lin of the Northern District of California granted Anthropic a preliminary injunction last week, directing that the administration desist from implementing the supply-chain risk designation or enforcing President Trump's directive that agencies in the executive branch wind down their use of Anthropic's AI models. 



  Lin ruled that the crackdown on Anthropic infringed on free-speech protections and seemed designed to retaliate against the company. 



  The administration had already indicated that it would appeal any injunction, according to The Wall Street Journal. 



  The dispute between Anthropic and the Pentagon stems from Anthropic's request that its models wouldn't be used by the Pentagon for fully autonomous weapons or domestic surveillance. That request led to an impasse in the parties' negotiations over Anthropic's contract with the Defense Department. 





  Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 02, 2026 11:48 ET (15:48 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Canada's Goods-Trade Deficit Widens to Six-Month High Even as Trade With Non-U.S. Nations Increases — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart 




  OTTAWA--Canada's trade deficit with the world surged to a six-month high as shipments of gold drove imports to record levels, though evidence is growing the country's dependence on the massive U.S. market is easing. 



  The country recorded a merchandise-trade deficit in February of 5.74 billion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of about US$4.14 billion, Statistics Canada said Thursday. That was much larger than the C$2.5 billion shortfall economists anticipated. 



  Imports jumped 8.4% between January and February to an all-time high of C$72.05 billion as inbound shipments of gold led widespread increases, outpacing a 6.4% rise in exports to C$66.31 billion, the highest since March of last year when the first wave of the Trump administration's tariffs kicked in. 



  Trade has been more volatile than usual for more than a year following the U.S. shift to more protectionist policies. Canada has racked up trade deficits five months running, and the C$9.92 billion gap in the first two months of 2026 marked a record year-to-date shortfall. 



  Although exports were up strongly in the latest month, climbing 5.2% in volume terms from a month earlier, that paled against a 9.4% surge in the volume of imports. Even if the balance flips in March, economists expect net trade will be a headwind to growth in the first quarter for an economy already showing strains in its labor market and a slowdown in housing activity. 



  Canada's cross border trade with the U.S., by far the country's biggest market, picked up in February. As did two-way trade with countries beyond America, with both exports to and imports from non-U.S. nations hitting records for the month. The result was Canada's longstanding goods-trade surplus with its neighbor continuing to narrow, hitting its smallest since May 2020 at C$1.73 billion, even as the deficit with all other countries narrowed to C$7.48 billion. 



  Former central banker Mark Carney was elected to office last April as a counter to President Trump, with pledges to decouple Canada's dependence on the U.s. and double non-U.S. exports in the next decade. The prime minister and government officials have made a series of trips to China, India, Europe and elsewhere in an effort to find new markets for the country's energy, minerals and other goods. Data earlier this week from the national statistics agency showed exports of crude oil to countries other than the U.S. hit a fresh record in January and shipments of natural gas were the highest since the country began exporting from a terminal on the west coast in mid-2025. 



  Canada's economy is on track to show modest growth in the first quarter of the year, before the full shock of higher oil prices due to the Iran war is felt. Gross domestic product edged up 0.1% in January from a month earlier as growth in oil and gas extraction and in construction helped to counter a pullback in manufacturing, and advance data from Statistics Canada indicated the pace accelerated to 0.2% growth in February as manufacturing recovered. 



  Exchanges of gold played a big part in the swings in trade in February. Excluding precious metals and their alloys, which for Canada is largely unwrought gold, exports for the month were up 5.5% and imports rose 5.8%. 



  Imports were up across most categories of goods, albeit led by record imports of gold with a jump in purchases from the U.S. Coinciding with a rebound in Canadian auto production following extended winter shutdowns at Ontario assembly plants, imports of motor vehicles and parts were up strongly. Canada's auto-manufacturing industry is deeply integrated with the U.S., but has felt the pressure of tariffs and trade policy uncertainty. 



  Canadian exports of motor vehicles and parts soared 24.2% in February following a slump the month before. Precious-metals shipments from Canada also were up strongly, led by a rise in gold exports to the U.K. Exports of unwrought aluminum recovered from a drop at the start of the year, and there was a rise in agricultural shipments to China following an agreement in January that saw China lower most tariffs on Canadian canola in exchange for allowing a limited number of Chinese electric vehicles to be brought into Canada. 



  "Despite a wider deficit and plenty of noise in both autos and gold, the broad-based increase in exports and imports is a positive for economic activity," said Shelly Kaushik, senior economist at Bank of Montreal Capital Markets. "While the China deal highlights some upside from efforts toward market diversification, overall activity will remain under pressure as long as the Canada-U.S. relationship is subject to elevated uncertainty. 



  Looking ahead, economists don't expect to see a similar jump in gold imports in March while exports will reflect the spike in crude oil prices, even as energy costs for consumers rise. 



  Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 02, 2026 11:49 ET (15:49 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Finite Carbon Announces Landmark Issuance of High-Quality Carbon Credits Under ACR IFM v2.1
data.description:
Finite Carbon Announces Landmark Issuance of High-Quality Carbon Credits Under ACR IFM v2.1

Credits issued over 200,000 acres in Maine meet the carbon market’s latest rigorous standards for quality and integrity

Finite Carbon (“Finite”), a leading developer of carbon projects, announces the issuance of forest carbon credits under the American Carbon Registry’s Improved Forest Management (IFM) v2.1 methodology for its 212,806-acre Northeast Carry Woodlands project in Maine. As one of the first and largest projects to achieve registration under ACR's newest IFM methodology, all Northeast Carry credits bear the Core Carbon Principles (CCP) label, the voluntary carbon market's designation for high-quality carbon credits.

The latest ACR IFM v2.1 methodology refines how carbon project baselines are evaluated and monitored. This improves project transparency and accountability, increasing confidence in nature-based climate solutions. By entering the voluntary carbon market, the Northeast Carry Woodlands project will provide long-term, quantifiable outcomes for carbon storage, responsible forest stewardship, and local economic stability.

"I'm proud of our team for this accomplishment," said David Stevenson, President, Finite Carbon. "This project underscores our dedication to providing high-integrity forest carbon credits. Using ACR’s robust IFM v2.1 methodology ensures measurable climate impact and supports healthy forest management across North America."

The Northeast Carry Woodlands project is part of a larger ownership of over 1 million acres in Maine’s expansive northern forest, under the care of the Buck family. Their vision of sustainable forest stewardship will allow this land to endure for future generations while supporting the local economy of one of the largest private forest landscapes in the eastern United States. This biodiverse landscape features a mix of northern hardwood and softwood forests, rivers, lakes, and wetlands, including Moosehead Lake, the Penobscot River, and the Allagash Wilderness Waterway.

“Since this project’s inception, our goals have remained consistent in every step necessary to achieve unquestionable quality. This project demonstrates our commitment to responsible, sustainable forest stewardship and delivers the highest integrity carbon solutions to the market,” said Ben Benoit, manager of the Buck Family’s timber lands.

The Northeast Carry Woodlands project is certified under the Sustainable Forestry Initiative (SFI), an independent certification program for sustainable forest management. The project exceeds the Maine Forest Service Best Management Practices for water quality by maintaining enhanced forest cover along riparian zones, protecting waterways, reducing soil erosion, and mitigating flooding.

Project Details:

data.title: Options traders are bracing for wild stock-market swings as Trump keeps investors guessing on Iran
data.description:
By Gordon Gottsegen

UBS identified record positioning in both long calls and short puts existing in tandem

The S&P 500 has seen both sharp rallies and significant declines over the past two weeks.

If you're having trouble figuring out which direction the stock market moves from here, you're not alone.

Options traders, worried about the lack of clarity surrounding the Iran conflict but not wanting to miss out on any gains from a sudden resolution that spurs a major stock-market turnaround, have been hedging their books aggressively in both directions using contracts tied to the S&P 500 SPX.

Options data from UBS showed institutional buy-side investors were piling into both calls and puts, meaning traders are bracing for large market swings. Calls typically pay off if the underlying index or asset rises in value, while puts do the opposite.

Data from UBS Derivatives Strategy pointed to almost $20 billion delta in net positioning for S&P 500 call options, and more than minus $80 billion delta in net positioning for puts. UBS said this reflects the degree of uncertainty in markets right now and that investors are well-positioned for both the upside and downside. In this instance, "delta" represents the sensitivity to the underlying - meaning a 1% move in the S&P 500 in either direction will affect the value of the contracts as if they represented a long position in the index worth $20 billion, or a short position worth $80 billion.

"You essentially have an environment where investors are very well hedged for uncertainty, particularly to the downside, but also via 'upside hedges,' positioning for a potential snapback rally - as we witnessed a couple days ago," Maxwell Grinacoff, head of U.S. equity derivatives research at UBS Securities, told MarketWatch.

Grinacoff noted that the significant amount of options buying means that market makers and dealers have to respond by hedging their exposure on the other side of the trade. That means buying into stock-market rallies and selling into dips, which in turn exacerbates moves in both directions.

Grinacoff also pointed out that this dynamic is the opposite of what happened when markets sold off in response to President Donald Trump's "liberation day" tariffs, which were announced in early April 2025. After April, demand for options meant that market makers and dealers had to buy dips and sell rallies to hedge, which he said helped suppress some volatility in the market.

With that trend reversed, the current market setup has left room for volatility VIX to move higher, so investors may not be out of the woods just yet.

See: Stock futures sink as Trump says U.S. on track to complete Iran objectives 'very shortly'

U.S. stocks were swinging both lower and higher on Thursday after Trump reiterated during a speech to the country on Wednesday evening that the Iran war would be over soon. But his comments offered few new details about plans for winding down the conflict.

-Gordon Gottsegen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  04-02-26 1139ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: OneLegacy Celebrates Donate Life Month with Community Events Across Southern California
data.description:
OneLegacy Celebrates Donate Life Month with Community Events Across Southern California

This April, OneLegacy, the nonprofit organ procurement organization serving seven counties in Southern California, joins hundreds of organizations nationwide to honor National Donate Life Month with a series of events aimed at inspiring more people to register as organ, eye, and tissue donors.

More than 100,000 people in the United States are currently waiting for a lifesaving organ transplant, including more than 20,000 in California. Behind every number is a person, a parent, child, or a friend, waiting for a second chance at life, yet fewer than 1% of deaths meet the criteria for organ donation. A single organ donor can save up to eight lives and enhance the lives of up to 75 others through cornea and tissue donation.

National Donate Life Month recognizes the generosity of donors and their families while highlighting the urgent need for more registered donors. Throughout April, OneLegacy will host dozens of outreach, education, and awareness events across Southern California, including:

  • OneLegacy Donate Life Run/Walk – OneLegacy will host its 24th annual Donate Life Run/Walk at Azusa Pacific University on Saturday, April 25. More than 3,000 participants are expected to attend, celebrating the gift of life alongside donor families, recipients, and supporters. Registration is available at www.OneLegacyRunWalk.org.
  • Tower Buzzers MiLB Baseball Game – OneLegacy will partner with the Ontario Tower Buzzers, Minor League Baseball affiliate of the Los Angeles Dodgers, for a Community Spotlight game on Thursday, April 16, at 6:35 p.m. at ONT Field. Fans can purchase tickets through this link, with 50% of proceeds supporting OneLegacy’s mission. The event will include special giveaways and a ceremonial first pitch.
  • Flags Across America – OneLegacy will collaborate with more than 200 hospitals, transplant centers, and community partners to raise the Donate Life flag throughout April. This national initiative, launched in 2009, honors donors and raises awareness about the importance of organ donation. OneLegacy will participate in more than 25 flag-raising ceremonies across its service area.
  • Blue and Green Spirit Week (April 4–11) – OneLegacy invites the community to show support by wearing blue and green. On April 11, hospitals, transplant centers, and community partners will host local events, while city halls and landmarks across Southern California will be illuminated in blue and green throughout the month.
  • Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) Partnership – For more than 20 years, OneLegacy has partnered with local DMV offices to provide education about organ donation. In 2026, the organization will continue outreach efforts at dozens of DMV locations, focusing on communities with the lowest donor registration rates.
  • LAFF4LIFE Comedy Events for Registered Donors – Throughout April, registered organ donors can receive free tickets to comedy shows at IMPROV and Levity Live locations nationwide. LAFF4LIFE, founded by double-organ recipient and comedian Jamie Alcroft, continues to raise awareness through entertainment. Learn more at improv.com/donors and levitylive.com/donors.
  • City Proclamations – OneLegacy Ambassadors will partner with local leaders to secure Donate Life Month proclamations across Southern California. More than 100 ambassadors will share their personal stories and help raise awareness in their communities.

“My son Ivan Sandoval was an organ donor and he saved three lives,” says OneLegacy Ambassador, Regina Meaux who honors her five-year-old son’s memory at events throughout Donate Life Month. “It’s powerful to come out and tell our stories and connect with other people who have experienced the same thing and spread the word that organ, eye and tissue donation does save lives.”

“We look forward to expanding our presence in the communities we serve during Donate Life Month,” said Prasad Garimella, Chief Executive Officer at OneLegacy. “Through partnerships with hospitals, transplant centers, community organizations, and local leaders, we are working to inspire more people to register as donors and help save and heal lives.”

The need for organ donation transcends all communities, including differences in race, ethnicity, religion, and background. Registering as an organ, eye, and tissue donor is simple and can be done online at OneLegacy.org/register or by saying “yes” at your local DMV.

About OneLegacy

OneLegacy is the nonprofit organization dedicated to saving lives through organ, eye and tissue donation in Southern California, including Los Angeles, Orange, Riverside, San Bernardino, Ventura, Santa Barbara and Kern counties. Serving a diverse population of more than 20 million people, OneLegacy partners with over 200 hospitals and 9 transplant centers, to ensure that lifesaving organs and tissues are recovered and transplanted for waiting recipients. Make the choice today, become an organ, eye and tissue donor by registering online at OneLegacy.org/register or by “checking YES” at your local DMV or on your California Tax Returns. For more information, visit OneLegacy.org

Sarah E. Fahey
mediarelations@onelegacy.org
213-518-9820

data.title: MODALO Unveils German Handcrafted Luxury Watch Winders
data.description:
MODALO Unveils German Handcrafted Luxury Watch Winders

Luxury watch winders are no longer just functional devices—they are an extension of the timepieces they protect. MODALO, a German premium manufacturer, combines refined hand-finished craftsmanship with a wide range of designs and universal compatibility, creating watch winders that integrate seamlessly into sophisticated interiors and diverse watch collections.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260401415154/en/

Innovation at your fingertips: The state-of-the-art MODALO watch winder touch display showcases the brand's industry-leading technology and premium quality standards.

The Intersection of Fine Art and Engineering

For collectors, a watch winder must match the quality of the timepiece it holds. MODALO achieves this by combining master-grade craftsmanship with precise mechanical standards. Each unit features multi-layered piano lacquer finishes and is crafted from premium materials such as precious woods, mother-of-pearl, and carbon fiber. This dedication to hand-finished craftsmanship ensures that every system functions as both a technical instrument and a refined design object.

Durability Beyond Industry Standards

At the heart of every MODALO watch winder are durable, custom-developed motors engineered for long-term reliability. With high torque reserves, these drives ensure smooth and stable rotation even under demanding conditions. This robust construction supports timepieces weighing up to 400 grams (around 14 oz) while protecting delicate movement components from irregular load peaks.

Precision via Intelligent Sensors: The 12 O'Clock Stop Position

MODALO systems feature a sensor-controlled 12 o’clock stop position, ensuring the watch remains vertical during every rest phase. This allows for optimal presentation and readability while showcasing the timepiece in its most natural orientation.

Three Intelligent Control Concepts

MODALO’s technological approach is reflected in three operating concepts designed for precision, ease of use, and compatibility across automatic watches:

  • Precision Touch Display: Fine-tuning from 650 to 1,000 TPD (Turns Per Day), ensuring compatibility with virtually all automatic movements from leading manufacturers
  • Classic Rotary Control: Pre-configured programs from 900 to 1,700 TPD for intuitive operation
  • Intelligent Automatic Program: A calibrated 900 TPD mode for everyday use

Unlike many watch winders that approximate rotation cycles through timed intervals, MODALO systems count every single revolution—ensuring precise and consistent winding performance.

Detailed insights into these standards are available in the MODALO Technology Report.

About MODALO

Headquartered in Hamburg, Germany, MODALO has been manufacturing luxury watch winders and storage solutions for over two decades, combining German engineering with refined craftsmanship.

Experience the perfect synergy of German engineering and aesthetic design. Discover the full collection of premium watch winders, refined watch boxes, and luxury storage solutions at www.modalo.com.

media@modalo.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260401415154/en/
